eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus favors Nicolás Maduro remaining Venezuela's head of state by end-2026 at 64.5%, reflecting ongoing legal disputes where a Supreme Court order and some international bodies, including the UN, still recognize him as president despite his January 3 U.S. military capture and ongoing New York trial on charges. Delcy Rodríguez, his former vice president now serving as interim leader with military backing, holds 22.5% implied probability after recent power consolidation via purges of Maduro allies, cabinet appointments, and diplomatic outreach to Colombia and Barbados in late April. Opposition figure María Corina Machado trails at 8.5% amid calls for elections, which remain unscheduled, underscoring constitutional ambiguity over succession and transition timelines. U.S. sanction relief and resumed direct flights bolster Rodríguez's position short-term, but no major shift has resolved Maduro's formal status.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Chicago be between 38-39°F on May 4?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Chicago O'Hare Intl Airport Station in degrees Fahrenheit on 4 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Chicago O'Hare Intl Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/us/il/chicago/KORD.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Fahrenheit (eg, 21°F). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: National Weather Service guidance and forecast model consensus from GFS and ECMWF ensembles indicate a high near 52-55°F at Chicago O'Hare International Airport on May 4, driving the 99.3% market-implied probability for 48°F or higher amid seasonably cool but stable conditions. This follows recent highs of around 50°F on May 1 and 55°F on May 2 under a persistent upper-level trough suppressing temperatures well below the 66°F May 4 climatological normal. Diurnal heating under mostly sunny skies and light winds supports reliable warming from overnight lows in the upper 30s to low 40s. Realistic challenges include unexpected marine layer persistence from Lake Michigan or a sudden frontal reinforcement, though model agreement and minimal shower risk yield high trader confidence ahead of Sunday's final NWS update.

events.title: NK Lokomotiva Zagreb vs. HNK Rijeka
events.description: This event is for the upcoming Prva Liga game, scheduled for Sunday, May 3, 2026 between NK Lokomotiva Zagreb and HNK Rijeka.

eventMetadata.context_description: HNK Rijeka holds a slim 42.5% trader consensus as implied probability favorite for their HNL Round 33 clash at Lokomotiva Zagreb's Stadion Maksimir, driven by overwhelming head-to-head dominance—unbeaten in the last seven meetings (4W, 3D), including a 2-0 home win on March 1—and solid away form amid a tight table race, sitting fourth with 42 points from 31 matches versus Lokomotiva's 41 from 32 in fifth. Lokomotiva's recent defensive solidity (0.4 goals conceded per game over last five) and home advantage keep their 27.5% and draw's 28% viable in this closely contested matchup late in the season for top-six playoff spots. Rijeka contends with injuries to Samuele Vignato, Anel Husic (muscle), Téo Barisic (knee, season-ending), and others, but no fresh team news alters sentiment in the past 48 hours.

markets.question: Will South Korea’s 2026 Annual Inflation be between 1.5% and 1.7%?
markets.description:
This is a market about the variation of consumer prices in South Korea in the 12-month period ending December 2026, as reported by the South Korean Ministry of Data and Statistics (MODS).

This market will resolve according to the percentage change in South Korea’s Consumer Price Index (CPI) over the 12-month period ending December 2026 (December CPI % change compared to the same month of the previous year), according to the monthly MODS Consumer Price Survey report for the specified month.

The resolution source for this market will be the MODS Consumer Price Index monthly report released for December 2026, currently scheduled to be released on December 31, 2026. Resolution of this market will take place upon release of the aforementioned data. If no data for the specified month is released by the date the next month's data is scheduled to be released, this market will resolve based on data from the last available month.

The relevant report will be made available upon release at: https://mods.go.kr/board.es?mid=a20109020000&bid=11751&eng_board_type=01

Note: This market’s resolution source reports percentage change in the South Korean Consumer Price Index to only one decimal point (e.g. 2.0%). Thus this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving this market. For the full release schedule, see: https://mods.go.kr/schdl.es?mid=a20301000000

eventMetadata.context_description: Polymarket traders price South Korea's 2026 annual CPI inflation in a tight contest, with 2.7–2.9% leading at 41.1% implied probability versus 2.4–2.6% at 36.5%, reflecting March 2026 YoY headline inflation of 2.2%—up from February's 2.0% but undershooting forecasts of 2.4%—amid steady Bank of Korea policy rate at 2.50%. Core inflation eased to 2.2%, yet upside risks from surging March PPI (4.1% YoY), 16.1% import price spikes, and Mideast energy shocks have lifted forecasts, including IMF's 2.5% and OECD's 2.7% for the year. Key differentiators include April CPI data due May 6 and the BoK's May 28 meeting, which could signal rate path shifts based on global commodity pressures.

eventMetadata.context_description: Atlanta Braves ace Spencer Strider makes his 2026 season debut against Colorado Rockies lefty Kyle Freeland (1-2, 3.48 ERA) in Sunday's series finale at hitter-friendly Coors Field, where the surging Braves (24-10, first in NL East) seek a sweep after dominating 9-1 victories in the first two games behind Chris Sale's 11 strikeouts and Austin Riley's homer. Key injury concerns loom with Ronald Acuña Jr. exiting Saturday's win due to left hamstring tightness—his status uncertain—and Michael Harris II limited by a lingering quad strain, prompting recent bullpen recalls of Hunter Stratton and Anthony Molina. The Rockies (14-19, last in NL West) struggle amid thin pitching depth, though clear weather and altitude could boost offense for both sides. Trader consensus prices reflect Atlanta's franchise-record 11-0-1 start to series and road momentum.

markets.question: Will Citigroup fail by June 30, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the listed bank fails between market creation and June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No.”

For the purposes of this market, the listed bank will be considered to have “failed” if, within the listed date range, any of the following occurs under the bank’s applicable legal or regulatory framework:

- The listed bank’s primary banking regulator formally declares the institution insolvent or non-viable, or withdraws or revokes the bank’s license or authorization, and such determination initiates or directly results in resolution, liquidation, wind-down, or transfer actions.
- The listed bank enters a court-ordered liquidation, statutory resolution regime, or regulator-mandated wind-down, including the use of resolution tools such as bail-ins, forced asset transfers, or the establishment of a bridge bank.
- A government or resolution authority intervenes in a manner that wipes out or subordinates existing equity of the listed bank and transfers effective control of the bank to the state or a designated resolution authority, with continued operations dependent on official intervention.
- The listed bank publicly defaults on a payment obligation, including derivatives margin, repo, or physical commodity delivery, and such default is formally acknowledged by the bank’s primary regulator or resolution authority and directly results in the initiation of resolution, liquidation, license withdrawal, or regulator-mandated transfer of the bank.
- The listed bank is subject to a compulsory merger, acquisition, or transfer of all or substantially all of its assets and liabilities ordered or directed by its primary banking regulator or resolution authority due to the bank’s financial condition or to prevent failure, regardless of whether a formal insolvency declaration or immediate equity wipeout is publicly announced at the time of transfer.

If there is a potential failure of the listed bank within this market’s date range and a qualifying regulatory or court action has occurred but has not yet been fully published by the relevant authority, this market may remain open  to allow for confirmation. If no qualifying failure is confirmed by that date, this market will resolve to “No.”

The primary resolution source for this market will be official statements, filings, or actions by the listed bank’s primary banking regulator or resolution authority; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket assigns negligible implied probabilities—peaking at 2% for Royal Bank of Canada (RBC)—to failures among major listed banks by June 30, 2026, reflecting sector resilience amid two isolated 2026 FDIC resolutions of small institutions under $300 million in assets: Metropolitan Capital Bank & Trust (January 30) and Community Bank and Trust-West Georgia (May 1), both tied to idiosyncratic liquidity strains rather than systemic commercial real estate (CRE) distress. Aggregate unrealized securities losses of $306 billion remain buffered by $2.6 trillion in bank equity capital, with Q1 CRE delinquencies steady at 1.53% for large institutions passing recent Fed stress tests. Watch Q2 earnings reports, upcoming FOMC meetings, and FDIC problem bank disclosures (60 at 2025 year-end) for potential shifts in this closely watched, low-failure-risk landscape.

events.title: Vanraure Hachinohe FC vs. Thespa Gunma
events.description: This event is for the upcoming J2 100 Year Vision League game, scheduled for Sunday, May 3, 2026 between Vanraure Hachinohe FC and Thespa Gunma.

eventMetadata.context_description: Vanraure Hachinohe enters as trader consensus favorite at 56.5% implied probability in the J2/J3 100 Year Vision League East A matchup, bolstered by home advantage at Prifoods Stadium and a dominant head-to-head record—unbeaten in five meetings (4 wins, 1 draw, 9-1 aggregate), including a 1-0 away victory over Thespa Gunma in February. Currently 7th in the table versus Gunma's 9th, Vanraure's edge reflects stronger overall positioning despite a recent 4-1 away loss to Yokohama FC on April 29. Gunma's struggles continue post-relegation, with inconsistent away form contributing to balanced 26.5% odds for draw and visitors in this closely contested fixture.

markets.question: Will Nationalist Party win the most seats in the House of Representatives in the 2026 Maltese general election?
markets.description:
General elections are scheduled to be held in Malta on May 30, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the political party that wins the greatest number of seats in the House of Representatives (parliament of Malta) as a result of the 2026 Maltese general election.

If the results of this election are not known definitively by March 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

Parties will primarily be ranked by the number of seats won. In the event of a tie, the party with the greater number of valid votes will rank higher. If a tie persists, the party whose name appears first in alphabetical order will rank higher. This market will resolve to the party that occupies the highest finishing position after applying this ranking.

This market’s resolution will be based solely on the number of seats won by the named party in the House of Representatives in the 2026 Maltese general election, not any coalition it may be a part of.

This market will resolve based on the results of the 2026 Maltese general election, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve solely on the official results reported by the Government of Malta, such as those published by the Electoral Commission of Malta (https://electoral.gov.mt/).

markets.question: Will there be exactly 0 earthquakes of magnitude 6.5 or higher worldwide from April 27 - May 3?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the total number of earthquakes with a magnitude of 6.5 or higher that occur anywhere on Earth between April 27, 2026, 12:00 AM ET, and May 3, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

The resolution source for this market is the United States Geological Survey (USGS) Earthquake Hazards Program (https://earthquake.usgs.gov/earthquakes/browse/significant.php#sigdef).

If an earthquake of substantial size has occurred within this market's timeframe but not yet appeared on the resolution source, this market may remain open until May 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, or until the earthquake in question otherwise appears on the resolution source. If such an earthquake has not appeared on the resolution source by that date, another credible resolution source will be used.

This market may not resolve until the timeframe of this market has concluded. If a qualifying earthquake has been recorded on the final day, this market may remain open for 24 hours to allow for revisions to the recorded magnitude. After 24 hours, this market will resolve according to the latest provided data.

eventMetadata.context_description: **Trader consensus strongly favors zero magnitude 6.5 or greater earthquakes worldwide from April 27 to May 3, with an implied probability of 89.5%, driven by USGS monitoring confirming none have occurred through May 2 despite comprehensive global seismic networks tracking events in real time.** This positioning reflects the inherently random nature of seismicity—governed by tectonic stress accumulation along fault lines—where historical USGS data shows roughly 50–70 such quakes annually, averaging under one per week and yielding a baseline Poisson probability of about 40% for zero in seven days. The absence of aftershocks from recent M6+ events (latest April 26 off Japan) or emerging seismic swarms has further solidified this sentiment as the resolution window closes today, though a late-day event remains theoretically possible per USGS catalogs. Traders await final USGS updates for confirmation.

markets.question: Will Cleveland Browns win the 2026 AFC North?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the team that is declared as the AFC North division champion for the 2026 NFL regular season.

If multiple teams tie atop the division, this market will resolve according to the official tiebreak procedure of the NFL.

If the 2026 NFL regular season is cancelled, postponed after January 17, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, or there is otherwise no winner declared for this division within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the NFL; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Baltimore Ravens lead trader consensus at 49% implied probability to claim the AFC North crown, buoyed by their roster continuity under John Harbaugh, Lamar Jackson's MVP-caliber play, and a stout defense anchored by Roquan Smith, with the 2026 NFL Draft adding depth without major needs addressed. Cincinnati Bengals sit at 30% on Joe Burrow's elite quarterbacking and Ja'Marr Chase's explosiveness, though trading their first-round pick tempered draft upside. Cleveland Browns' 12% reflects post-draft optimism from revamping the offense with picks like Mason Graham and Quinton Judkins amid Deshaun Watson's contract overhang. Pittsburgh Steelers trail at 7%, hampered by quarterback uncertainty despite third-round selection Drew Allar and defensive strengths. Recent draft hauls and power rankings solidify Ravens' edge in this competitive division.
